rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERALD R. BARRICK
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (Undeveloped Land In Novato, Marin County, Ca)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. In January 2023, the Court issued an order confirming sale of the same property to a different purchaser. The order has not been vacated based on the confirmed purchaser's default under Probate Code section 10350, nor has the order been set aside under CCP section 473(b) absent the confirmed purchaser's default (see Law Revision Commission Comments to section 10316). Notice to vacate the prior sale order must be given to the purchaser and his broker named in the order. See section 10350(b). Once the prior sale order is vacated, a new report of sale must be filed under section 10350(d), although the administrator of this estate having full IAEA authority is free to proceed with resale without court confirmation. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/3/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been submitted or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) There was no Supplemental I&A filed in this case, only the Final filed on 10/5/22 (not 10/10/22) and a Reappraisal for Sale filed on 10/3/22. (It is not clear why a Reappraisal for Sale was filed when the sale was not confirmed by the Court) (4) The accounting begins with $950,000, but the value of the estate was $440,000 on the date of death, as the administrator himself alleges correctly in paragraph 7 of his petition. (5) Please review Probate Code Section 1062 regarding how DETAILED schedules should be presented. (6) The 7/5/22 order for final distribution in the predeceased husband's estate ordered the personal representative to deposit $3,611.28 from that estate's blocked account into a blocked account for this estate, not $3,586.31 as shown in the (incomplete) accounting. (7) No receipt for the blocked account appears to have been filed in this case for that transfer. (8) Why is there a $28,590.74 "confirmation of assets" from the estate of the post-deceased son's estate? What does this represent? His estate should be receiving a share of this estate, not the other way around, unless he owed this decedent? (9) The closing statement does not fully confirm the costs of sale shown in the (incomplete) accounting: the notary fee and recording fees differ. And the prorated county taxes do not appear to be reflected in the accounting. (10) Bank statements verifying the ending balance of assets must encompass the ending date of the accounting, not precede it. (11) Based on the I&A and the seller's closing statement, the estate actually had a gain on sale $460,000 (12) Paragraph 15 of the petition includes costs to be reimbursed to the administrator and to his attorney, but these are not set forth anywhere or supported. (13) There were personal property taxes paid? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/20/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The attorney did not complete the Statement About the Bond on either I&A.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUM CHUEN TO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. APPEARANCE FOR NO RESPONSE: If a hearing is continued for issues raised by an examiner to be addressed and there is no response prior to the continued hearing date, the matter is automatically continued again, usually for one month. If there is still no response, an appearance is required. If no one appears after the automatic second continuance, the matter is ordinarily taken off calendar if it is not a compulsory petition, or an Order to Appear is issued. Issues posted for the hearing 7/19/23 still have not been addressed, and the attorney has not requested more time. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LARRY DEAN COATE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/03/2024 (no earlier date available), as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 10/27/2023) remain unaddressed: A supplement verified by the petitioner (and/or declarations of others having personal knowledge) addressing the following issues must be filed: i) What day of February 2017 did the petitioner see the decedent sign and acknowledge the will? ii) Jennifer Walters' complete zip code must be listed at item 8. iii) Arithinat Surawatthana's address listed at item 8 must match the address listed (in English) on the proof of service attached to the DE-121 form. iv) Attachment 2 must describe anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate, even if the total amount of such liabilities and claims does not exceed $20,000. v) If the petitioner is one of the only two witnesses to the will, allegations must be made to rebut the presumption under section 6112(c) that the petitioner procured her devise by duress, menace, fraud, or undue influence, as the issue may affect the petitioner's eligibility for appointment under sections 8402(a)(3) & 8502.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TERRY TINYEAH FANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/20/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) Item 8 of the petition must identify the trustee of the devisee trust. If the petitioner is the trustee, all beneficiaries of the trust must also be clearly identified as such and must receive notice. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/8/23: the form DE-147S; the petitioner's verified supplement to his petition, supplementing item 8; and the proof of service of notice with instructions for how to appear remotely attached. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
